t: @lasalleansunite @mussy03_ @MinutemenFA Jareer is a word to describe type 4 hair, and SOME people use it as a derogatory word against Somali Bantus (which is just one tribe btw).
exp: This post explains that "jareer" is a word used to describe type 4 hair, and that it is also used as a derogatory term against Somali Bantus. It is an explanation of a slur, not the use of a slur to attack a group.

t:
The crisis in South Sudan is forcing families to flee.

Tens of thousands of people have been displaced inside South Sudan and to neighbouring countries.

Needs are rising fast. Support is not keeping pace.

Please don’t look away.
Donate if you can: https://t.co/ajPPPUxvt8 https://t.co/P8WpFjYxEK
exp: This post is a humanitarian appeal highlighting the crisis in South Sudan, the displacement of families, and the urgent need for support. It is a call to action for donations and contains no hate speech.

t:

By Madit Majak | South Sudan Eagle Media 

Today, May 16, 2026, marks another historic anniversary of the founding of the Sudan People’s Liberation Army/Movement (SPLA/M), a struggle that began in 1983 under the leadership of John Garang de Mabior and many
exp: This post commemorates SPLA Day, marking the historic anniversary of the founding of the Sudan People’s Liberation Army/Movement (SPLA/M). It is a historical and celebratory post, not hate speech.

t: Somalia’s Parliament mandate ended 14 Apr 2026 & the President’s term ended 15 May 2026, with no transition deal in place. HSM repeatedly affirmed this publicly & to intl partners. Any continuation without lawful consensus breaches legitimacy & risks serious national instability. https:/
exp: This post discusses the end of the Somali Parliament's and President's terms, noting the lack of a transition deal and the potential breach of legitimacy. This is a political commentary on governance and constitutional matters.

t: Waziri wa Gender Hanna Wendot Cheptumo yakhabola ari abandu 680 bakhafwa khulwa okhwirusia khubulamu emukulu wa Kenya hakari womwesi kwa June omwaka kuwere nende April omwaka kuno.#Amacheni https://t.co/lFhCDlxMIf
exp: This post, written in Luhya (a Kenyan language), reports that the Gender Minister Hanna Wendot Cheptumo stated that 680 people died by suicide in Kenya between June last year and April this year. The hashtag #Amacheni means 'news'. It is a factual news report.

t:
I strongly condemn the heinous attack carried out by unknown uniformed and ununiformed goons at Buhuru, Mumias West, where several innocent people were left injured.

This senseless bloodshed against innocent Kenyan civilians must come to an end. https://t.co/pmuz4SkDza
exp: The post condemns a violent attack carried out by 'goons' in Mumias West, Kenya, which resulted in injuries to innocent people. It calls for an end to such violence against Kenyan civilians. This is a condemnation of violence, not hate speech.

t: President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud, in a BBC interview before his term ended, said his mandate expires May 15, 2026. He now says he will serve until May 15, 2027, citing constitutional changes he previously said would be implemented after elections. https://t.co/oN2BYtkMR2
exp: This post reports on a political statement by President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud of Somalia regarding the extension of his term, citing constitutional changes. This is a news report about politics.

t:
South East State (Koonfur Bari) has been formally established following the successful conclusion of the Guulwade Conference (3–14 May 2026).

Mr Cumareey Cali has been elected State President, with a two-year mandate to lead the formation of state institutions, in line with the Federal Government’s
exp: This post reports on the formal establishment of a new state called 'South East State' (Koonfur Bari is Somali for South East) and the election of its president. This is a news report.

t:

Airstrikes wipe out RSF drone depot near Deleng and destroy militia sites in Um Siala

t:

Happy SPLA Day🇸🇸🤍 https://t.co/3ipl0IIPkv
exp: This post commemorates the heroes who fought for South Sudan's freedom, advocating for unity and justice. It contains no hate speech.

t: As Sudan’s devastating war enters its fourth year, there is little sign that the country’s warring factions are prepared to pursue peace. Both the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) continue to pursue military victory, rejecting repeated calls for a ceasefire and meaningf
exp: This post provides an update on the ongoing conflict in Sudan, noting the lack of progress towards peace between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). It is a factual report and does not contain hate speech.

t:
16th May 1983 is a very special day for South Sudan(ese). This day led to war that lasted for 21 years.

From 1983-2005, two million South Sudanese including some of those who started the struggle lost their lives.

In 2005, the famous Comprehensive Peace Agreement (CPA) was signed that led to refer
exp: This post provides a historical overview of May 16, 1983, marking the beginning of the 21-year war in South Sudan and its human cost. It is an informative historical account and does not contain hate speech.

t:
Remittances to Kenya eased to KSh 51.39Bn (US$ 397.78Mn) in April 2026, falling 5.9% YoY from US$ 422.89Mn in April 2025.

➠ North America: US$ 207.6Mn (KSh 26.8Bn) 
➠ Europe: US$ 81.8Mn (KSh 10.6Bn)
➠ Rest of World: US$ 108.4Mn (KSh 14.0Bn)

Jan-Apr 2026 sits at KSh 216.02Bn (US$ 1.67Bn), up 1.0% Y
exp: This post provides factual economic data regarding remittances to Kenya in April 2026, detailing the amounts from different regions. It is a financial report and does not contain hate speech.

t: @Kimuzi_ karibu ndugu, while you were away happened , welcome back
exp: This post is a friendly welcome message to a user, using the Swahili phrase "karibu ndugu" which means "welcome brother". It contains no hate speech.

t: Kenya received $397.8 million in diaspora remittances in April 2026, down 6.2 percent from March, but 12-month cumulative inflows crossed $5 billion — cementing transfers as a cornerstone of the country's foreign exchange earnings.  https://t.co/tZlOMiX36O
exp: This post reports on Kenya's diaspora remittances for April 2026, providing economic figures and highlighting their importance to the country's foreign exchange earnings. It is a factual economic report and does not contain hate speech.
